tactical_shape: Direct, straight play with long/looped balls and throw-ins as a source of goals
They dominated early, then became more cautious and let South Africa in
Looked to reassert after halftime but created little for long spells
standout_players: Michal Sadilek scored the opener after a throw-in move
Patrik Schick had an early chance and later had a header saved by Ronwen Williams
Adam Hlozek created the opener with his escape and cross
Matej Kovar dropped a ball to Thapelo Maseko but later the piece notes David Zima stopped a late shot, suggesting the defensive unit had to work
Pavel Sulc gave away the late penalty with a handball
home_read.momentum: A frustrating draw after a promising start; still alive, but the punditry suggests Czechia remain short of fluency and vulnerable despite having chances to win.

tactical_shape: More about rescue and survival than control; improved after a poor start and offered more in the second half
Used a direct approach at times, with chances coming from wide/right-side attacks and long-range efforts
standout_players: Teboho Mokoena scored the equaliser from the penalty spot
Oswin Appollis produced South Africa's first shot with a deflected effort that hit the side-netting and was their liveliest attacker for spells
Thapelo Maseko had a shot blocked after Kovar dropped the ball to him
Evidence Makgopa missed a late chance from close range
Aubrey Modiba forced a 96th-minute stop from David Zima
fatigue_injuries: Teboho Mokoena will miss the next game through a second yellow card
away_read.momentum: A rescue point that keeps them alive and lifts morale after the Mexico defeat, though they still need to improve to finish the job.
